title: W7.1 — LatencySimulator (lognormal distribution, seeded deterministic)
pyright_warnings_note: uv run pyright reports 0 errors on src/kairon/paper/latency.py and tests/paper/test_latency.py. The 0-error count is the W7.1 pyright --strict requirement. The existing ~700 informational warnings on pytest.approx and sklearn/sklearn-stubs are demoted per pyrightconfig.json.

pytest_regression.note: Baseline 524 reflects the W3 + W5 + W6 batch state. W7.1 adds 3 tests: test_deterministic_seeded, test_p50_p99_within_config, test_lognormal_shape. 524 + 3 = 527 passed, 16 skipped, 0 failed, 0 regressions.

latency_simulator.name: LatencySimulator
latency_simulator.config_class: LatencyConfig (frozen dataclass with mean_ms=50.0, sigma=0.5, max_ms=500.0, seed=20260608)
latency_simulator.rng: numpy.random.RandomState (legacy API; plan-pinned for forward-compat with W3-era fixtures)

methods.name: sample
methods.description: Draw a single round-trip latency in ms (clamped at max_ms)

methods.name: sample_many
methods.description: Draw n round-trip latencies in ms (clamped at max_ms)

methods.name: sample_raw
methods.description: Draw a single round-trip latency in ms (NOT clamped; power-user surface)

methods.name: _draw_unclamped / _draw_clamped
methods.description: Internals: lognormal draws with and without the max_ms clamp

lognormal_parameterisation.convention: v1 contract: (mean_ms, sigma) where mean_ms is the MEDIAN of the resulting lognormal (i.e. exp(mu) = mean_ms) and sigma is the underlying normal's std. Matches scipy.stats.lognorm(s=sigma, scale=mean_ms) so the W7.1 KS test in test_lognormal_shape can use scipy as the reference distribution.

ks_test_evidence.p_value: > 0.05 (the test asserts p > 0.05; achieved comfortably with a lognormal-shape match)

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: src/kairon/paper/latency.py created with LatencyConfig and LatencySimulator. The module is pure: no IO, no async, no global state. The v1 contract is (mean_ms=50, sigma=0.5, max_ms=500, seed=20260608). The legacy numpy RandomState API is plan-pinned for forward-compat with W3-era fixtures.

tests/paper/test_latency.py::test_deterministic_seeded passes. Two LatencySimulator instances with the same seed produce identical 100-sample sequences element-by-element (np.array_equal) and identical single-sample draws after the same number of cumulative draws. The v1 contract for paper-trader parity tests is preserved.

tests/paper/test_latency.py::test_p50_p99_within_config passes. A 1000-sample draw with seed=42 has p50 within 5% of mean_ms=50ms and p99 strictly below the max_ms=500ms cap. The clamp is the v1 contract; the test pins p99 < 500ms so a regression that disables the clamp is caught immediately.

tests/paper/test_latency.py::test_lognormal_shape passes. A 1000-sample draw from sample_raw (the unclamped power-user surface) passes a Kolmogorov-Smirnov two-sided test against scipy.stats.lognorm(s=0.5, scale=50.0) with p-value > 0.05. The 5% level is the canonical 'is this sample from the reference distribution?' check; the test guards against a parameterisation mistake (e.g. confusing mu with mean).
uv run pyright reports 0 errors on src/kairon/paper/latency.py and tests/paper/test_latency.py. The 0-error count is the W7.1 pyright --strict requirement.
uv run pytest --tb=short -q: 527 passed, 16 skipped, 0 failed (baseline 524 + 3 new W7.1 tests, 0 regressions). The latency module is additive to the paper package; no existing tests are affected.

deviation_from_spec.rng_legacy_api: Plan specifies numpy RandomState (legacy API). The implementation uses numpy.random.RandomState (the v1 contract) for forward-compat with the W3-era fixtures. A future story can switch to the newer Generator API without changing the public LatencySimulator surface.

notes: W7.1 ships the LatencySimulator with a lognormal distribution (mean_ms=50, sigma=0.5, max_ms=500) seeded by a numpy RandomState. The 3 new tests pin the W7.1 acceptance criteria: deterministic seeded, p50/p99 within config, and lognormal shape (KS test vs scipy). The clamped sample is the v1 contract (the max_ms=500 cap keeps the long-tail from leaking into the latency model); the raw (unclamped) sample is exposed as a power-user surface for future latency-aware sizing. The module is pure and additive; no existing tests are affected. W7.4 latency-aware sizer can consume the simulator's output.
